description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone block with a weathered, uneven surface and a prominent carved relief on one face. The front features a stylized animal head, possibly a bull, flanked by geometric patterns that suggest ancient or classical architectural motifs. The sides and back of the block are unadorned, showing natural stone texture with some chipping and irregularities. The top surface is relatively flat but not perfectly smooth, indicating it may have been used as a structural or decorative element. The stone appears dense and heavy, with a pale, off-white coloration and subtle reddish discolorations in some areas. Its overall appearance suggests historical significance, possibly as part of a temple, monument, or urban heritage site.
description: A heavy, pale stone block with a carved animal head and geometric relief on one face, showing signs of age and weathering.
description_view_0: The front view displays the detailed carved relief of an animal head and geometric patterns, making this the most ornate and visually significant side.
description_view_1: The left side is rough and unadorned, showing the natural stone texture and some surface irregularities.
description_view_2: The back is plain, with no carvings or decorations, and has a slightly uneven surface.
description_view_3: The right side is also unadorned, with visible roughness and minor discoloration.
attribute_location_context: ancient temple foundation or archaeological urban site
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of chipping, minor erosion, and some discoloration, indicating long-term exposure to the elements.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible carvings on the front face, including an animal head and geometric patterns.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely forms part of a continuous wall or decorative frieze.
